name: AUROC
definition: Area under the Receiver Operating Characteristic curve. Probability that a randomly chosen positive has higher predicted probability than a randomly chosen negative.
when_to_use: Binary classification model comparison, threshold-independent evaluation
limitations: Insensitive to calibration. Can be misleading with severe class imbalance. Does not reflect clinical utility.

name: AUPRC
definition: Area under the Precision-Recall curve. Summarizes the tradeoff between precision and recall at different thresholds.
when_to_use: Imbalanced datasets where prevalence < 5%. Rare event prediction (e.g., rare diagnoses).
limitations: Baseline depends on prevalence (unlike AUROC). Harder to compare across datasets with different prevalence.

name: Expected Calibration Error
definition: Weighted average of the absolute difference between predicted probability and observed frequency across bins.
when_to_use: Assessing whether predicted probabilities are trustworthy for clinical decision-making.
limitations: Sensitive to number and width of bins. Multiple binning schemes can give different results.

name: Brier Score
definition: Mean squared difference between predicted probabilities and actual binary outcomes. Decomposes into calibration + discrimination + uncertainty.
when_to_use: Combined assessment of calibration and discrimination. Survival models (Brier score over time).
limitations: Hard to interpret clinically. Decomposition needed to separate calibration from discrimination.

name: Net Benefit (DCA)
definition: Expected benefit of using the model minus expected harm, across a range of threshold probabilities. Compares model vs treat-all vs treat-none.
range: Can be negative (model is harmful) to positive (model adds value)
when_to_use: Clinical decision-making. Determining the threshold range where the model is useful.
limitations: Requires choosing clinically relevant threshold range. Assumes specific harm-to-benefit ratio at each threshold.
